speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Before Angiers well met, brave Austria.—

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Arthur, that great forerunner of thy blood,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Richard, that robbed the lion of his heart

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And fought the holy wars in Palestine,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: By this brave duke came early to his grave.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And, for amends to his posterity,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: At our importance hither is he come

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To spread his colors, boy, in thy behalf,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And to rebuke the usurpation

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Of thy unnatural uncle, English John.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Embrace him, love him, give him welcome hither.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: God shall forgive you Coeur de Lion’s death

speaker: ARTHUR
text: The rather that you give his offspring life,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Shadowing their right under your wings of war.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I give you welcome with a powerless hand

speaker: ARTHUR
text: But with a heart full of unstainèd love.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Welcome before the gates of Angiers, duke.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: A noble boy. Who would not do thee right?

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Upon thy cheek lay I this zealous kiss

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: As seal to this indenture of my love:

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: That to my home I will no more return

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Till Angiers and the right thou hast in France,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Together with that pale, that white-faced shore,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Whose foot spurns back the ocean’s roaring tides

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: And coops from other lands her islanders,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Even till that England, hedged in with the main,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: That water-wallèd bulwark, still secure

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: And confident from foreign purposes,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Even till that utmost corner of the West

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Salute thee for her king. Till then, fair boy,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Will I not think of home, but follow arms.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, take his mother’s thanks, a widow’s thanks,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Till your strong hand shall help to give him strength

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To make a more requital to your love.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: The peace of heaven is theirs that lift their swords

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: In such a just and charitable war.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Well, then, to work. Our cannon shall be bent

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Against the brows of this resisting town.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Call for our chiefest men of discipline

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To cull the plots of best advantages.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: We’ll lay before this town our royal bones,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Wade to the marketplace in Frenchmen’s blood,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: But we will make it subject to this boy.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Stay for an answer to your embassy,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Lest unadvised you stain your swords with blood.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My lord Chatillion may from England bring

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That right in peace which here we urge in war,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And then we shall repent each drop of blood

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That hot rash haste so indirectly shed.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: A wonder, lady! Lo, upon thy wish

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Our messenger Chatillion is arrived.—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: What England says say briefly, gentle lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: We coldly pause for thee. Chatillion, speak.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Then turn your forces from this paltry siege

speaker: CHATILLION
text: And stir them up against a mightier task.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: England, impatient of your just demands,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Hath put himself in arms. The adverse winds,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Whose leisure I have stayed, have given him time

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To land his legions all as soon as I.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: His marches are expedient to this town,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: His forces strong, his soldiers confident.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: With him along is come the Mother Queen,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: An Ate stirring him to blood and strife;

speaker: CHATILLION
text: With her her niece, the Lady Blanche of Spain;

speaker: CHATILLION
text: With them a bastard of the King’s deceased.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: And all th’ unsettled humors of the land—

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Rash, inconsiderate, fiery voluntaries,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: With ladies’ faces and fierce dragons’ spleens—

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Have sold their fortunes at their native homes,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Bearing their birthrights proudly on their backs,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To make a hazard of new fortunes here.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: In brief, a braver choice of dauntless spirits

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Than now the English bottoms have waft o’er

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Did never float upon the swelling tide

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To do offense and scathe in Christendom.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: The interruption of their churlish drums

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Cuts off more circumstance. They are at hand,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To parley or to fight, therefore prepare.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: How much unlooked-for is this expedition.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: By how much unexpected, by so much

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: We must awake endeavor for defense,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: For courage mounteth with occasion.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Let them be welcome, then. We are prepared.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Peace be to France, if France in peace permit

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Our just and lineal entrance to our own.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: If not, bleed France, and peace ascend to heaven,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Whiles we, God’s wrathful agent, do correct

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Their proud contempt that beats his peace to heaven.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Peace be to England, if that war return

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: From France to England, there to live in peace.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: England we love, and for that England’s sake

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: With burden of our armor here we sweat.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: This toil of ours should be a work of thine;

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: But thou from loving England art so far

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: That thou hast underwrought his lawful king,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Cut off the sequence of posterity,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Outfacèd infant state, and done a rape

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Upon the maiden virtue of the crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Look here upon thy brother Geoffrey’s face.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: These eyes, these brows, were molded out of his;

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: This little abstract doth contain that large

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Which died in Geoffrey, and the hand of time

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Shall draw this brief into as huge a volume.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: That Geoffrey was thy elder brother born,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And this his son. England was Geoffrey’s right,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And this is Geoffrey’s. In the name of God,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: How comes it then that thou art called a king,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: When living blood doth in these temples beat

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Which owe the crown that thou o’ermasterest?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: From whom hast thou this great commission, France,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To draw my answer from thy articles?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: From that supernal judge that stirs good thoughts

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In any breast of strong authority

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To look into the blots and stains of right.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: That judge hath made me guardian to this boy,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Under whose warrant I impeach thy wrong,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And by whose help I mean to chastise it.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Alack, thou dost usurp authority.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Excuse it is to beat usurping down.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Who is it thou dost call usurper, France?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Let me make answer: thy usurping son.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Out, insolent! Thy bastard shall be king

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: That thou mayst be a queen and check the world.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My bed was ever to thy son as true

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As thine was to thy husband, and this boy

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Liker in feature to his father Geoffrey

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Than thou and John, in manners being as like

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As rain to water or devil to his dam.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My boy a bastard? By my soul, I think

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: His father never was so true begot.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: It cannot be, an if thou wert his mother.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: There’s a good mother, boy, that blots thy father.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: There’s a good grandam, boy, that would blot thee.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Peace!

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hear the crier!

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: What the devil art thou?

speaker: BASTARD
text: One that will play the devil, sir, with you,

speaker: BASTARD
text: An he may catch your hide and you alone.

speaker: BASTARD
text: You are the hare of whom the proverb goes,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Whose valor plucks dead lions by the beard.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I’ll smoke your skin-coat an I catch you right.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sirrah, look to ’t. I’ faith, I will, i’ faith!

speaker: BLANCHE
text: O, well did he become that lion’s robe

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That did disrobe the lion of that robe.

speaker: BASTARD
text: It lies as sightly on the back of him

speaker: BASTARD
text: As great Alcides’ shoes upon an ass.—

speaker: BASTARD
text: But, ass, I’ll take that burden from your back

speaker: BASTARD
text: Or lay on that shall make your shoulders crack.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: What cracker is this same that deafs our ears

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: With this abundance of superfluous breath?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Louis, determine what we shall do straight.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Women and fools, break off your conference.—

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: King John, this is the very sum of all:

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: England and Ireland, Anjou, Touraine, Maine,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: In right of Arthur do I claim of thee.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Wilt thou resign them and lay down thy arms?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: My life as soon! I do defy thee, France.—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Arthur of Brittany, yield thee to my hand,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And out of my dear love I’ll give thee more

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Than e’er the coward hand of France can win.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Submit thee, boy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Come to thy grandam, child.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Do, child, go to it grandam, child.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Give grandam kingdom, and it grandam will

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Give it a plum, a cherry, and a fig.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: There’s a good grandam.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Good my mother, peace.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I would that I were low laid in my grave.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I am not worth this coil that’s made for me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: His mother shames him so, poor boy, he weeps.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Now shame upon you whe’er she does or no!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: His grandam’s wrongs, and not his mother’s shames,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Draws those heaven-moving pearls from his poor eyes,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which heaven shall take in nature of a fee.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Ay, with these crystal beads heaven shall be bribed

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To do him justice and revenge on you.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Thou monstrous slanderer of heaven and Earth!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou monstrous injurer of heaven and Earth,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Call not me slanderer. Thou and thine usurp

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: The dominations, royalties, and rights

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Of this oppressèd boy. This is thy eldest son’s son,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Infortunate in nothing but in thee.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thy sins are visited in this poor child.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: The canon of the law is laid on him,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Being but the second generation

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Removèd from thy sin-conceiving womb.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Bedlam, have done.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I have but this to say,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That he is not only plaguèd for her sin,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But God hath made her sin and her the plague

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: On this removèd issue, plagued for her,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And with her plague; her sin his injury,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Her injury the beadle to her sin,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: All punished in the person of this child

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And all for her. A plague upon her!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Thou unadvisèd scold, I can produce

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: A will that bars the title of thy son.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Ay, who doubts that? A will—a wicked will,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A woman’s will, a cankered grandam’s will.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Peace, lady. Pause, or be more temperate.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: It ill beseems this presence to cry aim

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To these ill-tunèd repetitions.—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Some trumpet summon hither to the walls

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: These men of Angiers. Let us hear them speak

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Whose title they admit, Arthur’s or John’s.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Who is it that hath warned us to the walls?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: ’Tis France, for England.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: England, for itself.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: You men of Angiers, and my loving subjects—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: You loving men of Angiers, Arthur’s subjects,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Our trumpet called you to this gentle parle—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For our advantage. Therefore hear us first.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: These flags of France that are advancèd here

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Before the eye and prospect of your town,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Have hither marched to your endamagement.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The cannons have their bowels full of wrath,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And ready mounted are they to spit forth

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Their iron indignation ’gainst your walls.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: All preparation for a bloody siege

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And merciless proceeding by these French

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Confronts your city’s eyes, your winking gates,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And, but for our approach, those sleeping stones,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That as a waist doth girdle you about,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: By the compulsion of their ordinance

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: By this time from their fixèd beds of lime

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Had been dishabited, and wide havoc made

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For bloody power to rush upon your peace.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But on the sight of us your lawful king,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Who painfully with much expedient march

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Have brought a countercheck before your gates

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To save unscratched your city’s threatened cheeks,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Behold, the French, amazed, vouchsafe a parle.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And now, instead of bullets wrapped in fire

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To make a shaking fever in your walls,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: They shoot but calm words folded up in smoke

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To make a faithless error in your ears,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Which trust accordingly, kind citizens,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And let us in. Your king, whose labored spirits

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Forwearied in this action of swift speed,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Craves harborage within your city walls.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: When I have said, make answer to us both.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Lo, in this right hand, whose protection

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Is most divinely vowed upon the right

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Of him it holds, stands young Plantagenet,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Son to the elder brother of this man,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And king o’er him and all that he enjoys.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: For this downtrodden equity we tread

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In warlike march these greens before your town,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Being no further enemy to you

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Than the constraint of hospitable zeal

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In the relief of this oppressèd child

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Religiously provokes. Be pleasèd then

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To pay that duty which you truly owe

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To him that owes it, namely, this young prince,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And then our arms, like to a muzzled bear

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Save in aspect, hath all offense sealed up.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Our cannons’ malice vainly shall be spent

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Against th’ invulnerable clouds of heaven,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And with a blessèd and unvexed retire,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: With unbacked swords and helmets all unbruised,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: We will bear home that lusty blood again

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Which here we came to spout against your town,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And leave your children, wives, and you in peace.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: But if you fondly pass our proffered offer,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: ’Tis not the roundure of your old-faced walls

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Can hide you from our messengers of war,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Though all these English and their discipline

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Were harbored in their rude circumference.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Then tell us, shall your city call us lord

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In that behalf which we have challenged it?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Or shall we give the signal to our rage

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And stalk in blood to our possession?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: In brief, we are the King of England’s subjects.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: For him, and in his right, we hold this town.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Acknowledge then the King and let me in.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: That can we not. But he that proves the King,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: To him will we prove loyal. Till that time

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Have we rammed up our gates against the world.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Doth not the crown of England prove the King?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And if not that, I bring you witnesses,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Twice fifteen thousand hearts of England’s breed—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Bastards and else.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To verify our title with their lives.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: As many and as wellborn bloods as those—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Some bastards too.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Stand in his face to contradict his claim.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Till you compound whose right is worthiest,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: We for the worthiest hold the right from both.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Then God forgive the sin of all those souls

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That to their everlasting residence,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Before the dew of evening fall, shall fleet

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In dreadful trial of our kingdom’s king.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Amen, amen.—Mount, chevaliers! To arms!

speaker: BASTARD
text: Saint George, that swinged the dragon and e’er since

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sits on ’s horseback at mine hostess’ door,

speaker: BASTARD
text:
Teach us some fence!
                 
                To
                 
                Austria
                .
               Sirrah, were I at home

speaker: BASTARD
text: At your den, sirrah, with your lioness,

speaker: BASTARD
text: I would set an ox head to your lion’s hide

speaker: BASTARD
text: And make a monster of you.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Peace! No more.

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, tremble, for you hear the lion roar.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Up higher to the plain, where we’ll set forth

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In best appointment all our regiments.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Speed, then, to take advantage of the field.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: It shall be so, and at the other hill

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Command the rest to stand. God and our right!

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: You men of Angiers, open wide your gates,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: And let young Arthur, Duke of Brittany, in,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Who by the hand of France this day hath made

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Much work for tears in many an English mother,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Whose sons lie scattered on the bleeding ground.

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Many a widow’s husband groveling lies

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Coldly embracing the discolored earth,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: And victory with little loss doth play

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Upon the dancing banners of the French,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Who are at hand, triumphantly displayed,

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: To enter conquerors and to proclaim

speaker:
FRENCH
               
              HERALD
text: Arthur of Brittany England’s king and yours.

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Rejoice, you men of Angiers, ring your bells!

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: King John, your king and England’s, doth approach,

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Commander of this hot malicious day.

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Their armors, that marched hence so silver bright,

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Hither return all gilt with Frenchmen’s blood.

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: There stuck no plume in any English crest

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: That is removèd by a staff of France.

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Our colors do return in those same hands

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: That did display them when we first marched forth,

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: And like a jolly troop of huntsmen come

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Our lusty English, all with purpled hands,

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Dyed in the dying slaughter of their foes.

speaker:
ENGLISH
               
              HERALD
text: Open your gates, and give the victors way.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Heralds, from off our towers we might behold

speaker: CITIZEN
text: From first to last the onset and retire

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Of both your armies, whose equality

speaker: CITIZEN
text: By our best eyes cannot be censurèd.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Blood hath bought blood, and blows have answered blows,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Strength matched with strength, and power confronted power.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Both are alike, and both alike we like.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: One must prove greatest. While they weigh so even,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: We hold our town for neither, yet for both.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: France, hast thou yet more blood to cast away?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Say, shall the current of our right roam on,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Whose passage, vexed with thy impediment,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Shall leave his native channel and o’erswell

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: With course disturbed even thy confining shores,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Unless thou let his silver water keep

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A peaceful progress to the ocean?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: England, thou hast not saved one drop of blood

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In this hot trial more than we of France,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Rather lost more. And by this hand I swear

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: That sways the earth this climate overlooks,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Before we will lay down our just-borne arms,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: We’ll put thee down, ’gainst whom these arms we bear,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Or add a royal number to the dead,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Gracing the scroll that tells of this war’s loss

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: With slaughter coupled to the name of kings.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Ha, majesty! How high thy glory towers

speaker: BASTARD
text: When the rich blood of kings is set on fire!

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, now doth Death line his dead chaps with steel,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The swords of soldiers are his teeth, his fangs,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And now he feasts, mousing the flesh of men

speaker: BASTARD
text: In undetermined differences of kings.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Why stand these royal fronts amazèd thus?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Cry havoc, kings! Back to the stainèd field,

speaker: BASTARD
text: You equal potents, fiery-kindled spirits.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Then let confusion of one part confirm

speaker: BASTARD
text: The other’s peace. Till then, blows, blood, and death!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Whose party do the townsmen yet admit?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Speak, citizens, for England. Who’s your king?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: The King of England, when we know the King.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Know him in us, that here hold up his right.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In us, that are our own great deputy

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And bear possession of our person here,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Lord of our presence, Angiers, and of you.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: A greater power than we denies all this,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And till it be undoubted, we do lock

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Our former scruple in our strong-barred gates,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Kings of our fear, until our fears resolved

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Be by some certain king purged and deposed.

speaker: BASTARD
text: By heaven, these scroyles of Angiers flout you, kings,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And stand securely on their battlements

speaker: BASTARD
text: As in a theater, whence they gape and point

speaker: BASTARD
text: At your industrious scenes and acts of death.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your royal presences, be ruled by me:

speaker: BASTARD
text: Do like the mutines of Jerusalem,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Be friends awhile, and both conjointly bend

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your sharpest deeds of malice on this town.

speaker: BASTARD
text: By east and west let France and England mount

speaker: BASTARD
text: Their battering cannon chargèd to the mouths,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Till their soul-fearing clamors have brawled down

speaker: BASTARD
text: The flinty ribs of this contemptuous city.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I’d play incessantly upon these jades,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Even till unfencèd desolation

speaker: BASTARD
text: Leave them as naked as the vulgar air.

speaker: BASTARD
text: That done, dissever your united strengths

speaker: BASTARD
text: And part your mingled colors once again;

speaker: BASTARD
text: Turn face to face and bloody point to point.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Then in a moment Fortune shall cull forth

speaker: BASTARD
text: Out of one side her happy minion,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To whom in favor she shall give the day

speaker: BASTARD
text: And kiss him with a glorious victory.

speaker: BASTARD
text: How like you this wild counsel, mighty states?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Smacks it not something of the policy?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Now by the sky that hangs above our heads,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I like it well. France, shall we knit our powers

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And lay this Angiers even with the ground,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Then after fight who shall be king of it?

speaker: BASTARD
text: An if thou hast the mettle of a king,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Being wronged as we are by this peevish town,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Turn thou the mouth of thy artillery,

speaker: BASTARD
text: As we will ours, against these saucy walls,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And when that we have dashed them to the ground,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Why, then, defy each other and pell-mell

speaker: BASTARD
text: Make work upon ourselves, for heaven or hell.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Let it be so. Say, where will you assault?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: We from the west will send destruction

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Into this city’s bosom.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: I from the north.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Our thunder from the south

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Shall rain their drift of bullets on this town.

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, prudent discipline! From north to south,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Austria and France shoot in each other’s mouth.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I’ll stir them to it. — Come, away, away!

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Hear us, great kings. Vouchsafe awhile to stay,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And I shall show you peace and fair-faced league,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Win you this city without stroke or wound,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Rescue those breathing lives to die in beds

speaker: CITIZEN
text: That here come sacrifices for the field.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Persever not, but hear me, mighty kings.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Speak on with favor. We are bent to hear.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: That daughter there of Spain, the Lady Blanche,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Is near to England. Look upon the years

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Of Louis the Dauphin and that lovely maid.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: If lusty love should go in quest of beauty,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Where should he find it fairer than in Blanche?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: If zealous love should go in search of virtue,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Where should he find it purer than in Blanche?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: If love ambitious sought a match of birth,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Whose veins bound richer blood than Lady Blanche?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Such as she is, in beauty, virtue, birth,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Is the young Dauphin every way complete.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: If not complete of, say he is not she,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And she again wants nothing, to name want,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: If want it be not that she is not he.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: He is the half part of a blessèd man,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Left to be finishèd by such as she,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And she a fair divided excellence,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Whose fullness of perfection lies in him.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: O, two such silver currents when they join

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Do glorify the banks that bound them in,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And two such shores to two such streams made one,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Two such controlling bounds shall you be, kings,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: To these two princes, if you marry them.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: This union shall do more than battery can

speaker: CITIZEN
text: To our fast-closèd gates, for at this match,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: With swifter spleen than powder can enforce,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: The mouth of passage shall we fling wide ope

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And give you entrance. But without this match,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: The sea enragèd is not half so deaf,

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Lions more confident, mountains and rocks

speaker: CITIZEN
text: More free from motion, no, not Death himself

speaker: CITIZEN
text: In mortal fury half so peremptory

speaker: CITIZEN
text: As we to keep this city.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Here’s a stay

speaker: BASTARD
text: That shakes the rotten carcass of old Death

speaker: BASTARD
text: Out of his rags! Here’s a large mouth indeed

speaker: BASTARD
text: That spits forth death and mountains, rocks and seas;

speaker: BASTARD
text: Talks as familiarly of roaring lions

speaker: BASTARD
text: As maids of thirteen do of puppy dogs.

speaker: BASTARD
text: What cannoneer begot this lusty blood?

speaker: BASTARD
text: He speaks plain cannon fire, and smoke, and bounce.

speaker: BASTARD
text: He gives the bastinado with his tongue.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Our ears are cudgeled. Not a word of his

speaker: BASTARD
text: But buffets better than a fist of France.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Zounds, I was never so bethumped with words

speaker: BASTARD
text: Since I first called my brother’s father Dad.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Son, list to this conjunction; make this match.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Give with our niece a dowry large enough,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: For by this knot thou shalt so surely tie

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Thy now unsured assurance to the crown

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: That yon green boy shall have no sun to ripe

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: The bloom that promiseth a mighty fruit.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: I see a yielding in the looks of France.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Mark how they whisper. Urge them while their souls

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Are capable of this ambition,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Lest zeal, now melted by the windy breath

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Of soft petitions, pity, and remorse,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Cool and congeal again to what it was.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Why answer not the double majesties

speaker: CITIZEN
text: This friendly treaty of our threatened town?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Speak England first, that hath been forward first

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To speak unto this city. What say you?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: If that the Dauphin there, thy princely son,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Can in this book of beauty read “I love,”

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Her dowry shall weigh equal with a queen.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For Anjou and fair Touraine, Maine, Poitiers,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And all that we upon this side the sea—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Except this city now by us besieged—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Find liable to our crown and dignity,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Shall gild her bridal bed and make her rich

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In titles, honors, and promotions,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: As she in beauty, education, blood,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Holds hand with any princess of the world.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: What sayst thou, boy? Look in the lady’s face.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I do, my lord, and in her eye I find

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: A wonder or a wondrous miracle,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: The shadow of myself formed in her eye,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Which, being but the shadow of your son,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Becomes a sun and makes your son a shadow.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I do protest I never loved myself

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Till now infixèd I beheld myself

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Drawn in the flattering table of her eye.

speaker: BASTARD
text: “Drawn in the flattering table of her eye”?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hanged in the frowning wrinkle of her brow

speaker: BASTARD
text: And quartered in her heart! He doth espy

speaker: BASTARD
text: Himself love’s traitor. This is pity now,

speaker: BASTARD
text: That hanged and drawn and quartered there should be

speaker: BASTARD
text: In such a love so vile a lout as he.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: My uncle’s will in this respect is mine.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: If he see aught in you that makes him like,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That anything he sees which moves his liking

speaker: BLANCHE
text: I can with ease translate it to my will.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Or if you will, to speak more properly,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: I will enforce it eas’ly to my love.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Further I will not flatter you, my lord,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That all I see in you is worthy love,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Than this: that nothing do I see in you,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Though churlish thoughts themselves should be your judge,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That I can find should merit any hate.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What say these young ones? What say you, my niece?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That she is bound in honor still to do

speaker: BLANCHE
text: What you in wisdom still vouchsafe to say.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Speak then, Prince Dauphin. Can you love this lady?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Nay, ask me if I can refrain from love,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: For I do love her most unfeignedly.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Then do I give Volquessen, Touraine, Maine,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Poitiers and Anjou, these five provinces

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: With her to thee, and this addition more:

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Full thirty thousand marks of English coin.—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Philip of France, if thou be pleased withal,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Command thy son and daughter to join hands.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: It likes us well.—Young princes, close your hands.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: And your lips too, for I am well assured

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: That I did so when I was first assured.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Now, citizens of Angiers, ope your gates.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Let in that amity which you have made,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: For at Saint Mary’s Chapel presently

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: The rites of marriage shall be solemnized.—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Is not the Lady Constance in this troop?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: I know she is not, for this match made up

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Her presence would have interrupted much.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Where is she and her son? Tell me, who knows.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: She is sad and passionate at your Highness’ tent.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And by my faith, this league that we have made

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Will give her sadness very little cure.—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Brother of England, how may we content

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: This widow lady? In her right we came,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Which we, God knows, have turned another way

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To our own vantage.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: We will heal up all,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For we’ll create young Arthur Duke of Brittany

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And Earl of Richmond, and this rich, fair town

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: We make him lord of.—Call the Lady Constance.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Some speedy messenger bid her repair

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text:
To our solemnity.
                 
                Salisbury
                 
                exits
                .
               I trust we shall,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: If not fill up the measure of her will,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Yet in some measure satisfy her so

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That we shall stop her exclamation.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Go we as well as haste will suffer us

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To this unlooked-for, unpreparèd pomp.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Mad world, mad kings, mad composition!

speaker: BASTARD
text: John, to stop Arthur’s title in the whole,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hath willingly departed with a part;

speaker: BASTARD
text: And France, whose armor conscience buckled on,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Whom zeal and charity brought to the field

speaker: BASTARD
text: As God’s own soldier, rounded in the ear

speaker: BASTARD
text: With that same purpose-changer, that sly devil,

speaker: BASTARD
text: That broker that still breaks the pate of faith,

speaker: BASTARD
text: That daily break-vow, he that wins of all,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Of kings, of beggars, old men, young men, maids—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Who having no external thing to lose

speaker: BASTARD
text: But the word “maid,” cheats the poor maid of that—

speaker: BASTARD
text: That smooth-faced gentleman, tickling Commodity,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Commodity, the bias of the world—

speaker: BASTARD
text: The world, who of itself is peisèd well,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Made to run even upon even ground,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Till this advantage, this vile-drawing bias,

speaker: BASTARD
text: This sway of motion, this Commodity,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Makes it take head from all indifferency,

speaker: BASTARD
text: From all direction, purpose, course, intent.

speaker: BASTARD
text: And this same bias, this Commodity,

speaker: BASTARD
text: This bawd, this broker, this all-changing word,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Clapped on the outward eye of fickle France,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hath drawn him from his own determined aid,

speaker: BASTARD
text: From a resolved and honorable war

speaker: BASTARD
text: To a most base and vile-concluded peace.

speaker: BASTARD
text: And why rail I on this Commodity?

speaker: BASTARD
text: But for because he hath not wooed me yet.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Not that I have the power to clutch my hand

speaker: BASTARD
text: When his fair angels would salute my palm,

speaker: BASTARD
text: But for my hand, as unattempted yet,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Like a poor beggar raileth on the rich.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Well, whiles I am a beggar, I will rail

speaker: BASTARD
text: And say there is no sin but to be rich;

speaker: BASTARD
text: And being rich, my virtue then shall be

speaker: BASTARD
text: To say there is no vice but beggary.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Since kings break faith upon Commodity,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Gain, be my lord, for I will worship thee!
